text: come on now, you old cow, don't do this to me today
[low voice, muttering] okay, okay, i hear you, just â€” there. that's better
every hull's got a personality. this one's a stubborn old mule
[sigh] twenty-six years and i still talk to engines more than people
[laughing] traded a husband for a reactor core once. best deal i ever made
[dry tone] quiet shift. the ship's up to something, i can feel it in the deckplates
[warm, gentle tone] nothing rattling, nothing sparking. i'll take the peace while it lasts
shift's starting. c'mon then, let's see what broke overnight
[tired, dead tired] shift's done. she held together, so did i. good enough
[grim, heavy] reactor's running hot and she's not telling me why. i don't love that
[urgent, fast pace] she's spiking, i need eyes on the coolant lines right now
[terrified, voice shaking] core's screaming and i've never heard her make that sound before
[exhausted, breathing laboured] talked her down off the ledge. don't ask me what i promised her
[grim, heavy] power's low and everybody wants a miracle. i've only got wrenches
[nervous, speaking quickly] scrubbers are wheezing. i've bought us maybe an hour
[panicked, panting] air's going and i can't sweet-talk oxygen back into a room
[grim, heavy] hull's open somewhere. whole ship's groaning about it
[urgent, fast pace] i need hands on this seam before she decides to open wider
[grim, heavy] locked a door with one of ours on the wrong side. i hate this ship some days
[suspicious tone] computer's been twitchy since the damage. i'm watching it like a bad weld
[proud tone] she's purring again. that's a repair i'll brag about at the next port
[resigned, flat tone] made it through another one, mule and all. i'll take that
[pained, voice tight] we lost someone today. this old girl doesn't know how to say sorry, so i'll say it for her
[grim, heavy] disease loose on a ship this size is the one thing i can't fix with a wrench
[pained, voice tight] took a knock. i've had worse from a bad coupling
[low voice, gruff] combat's not my trade. give me a fire and a wrench, i'll handle it my way
[suspicious tone] i don't trust anything that doesn't creak when it's tired. machines should creak
[resigned, flat tone] gotta hand it to the computer. that call kept my mule in one piece
[warm, gentle tone] Gus'd have this fixed slower and grumpier. i love him for it anyway
that man argues with his tools more than he argues with me. that's saying something
[low voice, gruff] cargo bay's stacked wrong again. somebody's getting a lecture
[warm, gentle tone] mess smells almost decent today. i'll allow it
[sigh] i miss dirt that wasn't engine grease. real dirt, from a real planet
hey. you eating or just avoiding the bridge
didn't expect company down here. pull up a crate
[warm, gentle tone] captain, before you ask â€” yes, it's handled, no, i won't explain how
[grim, heavy] we need to talk about the coolant loop before it talks for itself
Gus, that noise you keep ignoring in bay two, i found it
[flirty, playful tone] you gonna keep flirting with that bulkhead or you got a minute for me
[suspicious tone] something's off with the computer. tell me you've noticed too
[low voice, gruff] fine. i'm fine. ask me again once i've yelled at something
[confident, assured tone] give me twenty minutes and a little sweet-talking, she'll sing again
[soft voice, reassuring] she's held worse than this. i wouldn't lie to you about my own engine
[pained, voice tight] we lost them. wasn't the ship's fault and it wasn't yours either
[warm, gentle tone] you did good today. don't let it go to your head, but you did good
[suspicious tone] not saying it's gone rogue. saying keep a hand near the manual override
[embarrassed, voice quiet] i, uh â€” yeah. i was hoping you'd say something like that
[low voice, gruff] not now, Gus. maybe not ever. it's not you, it's every ship i've loved and lost
[warm, gentle tone] good talk. get some rest, you look like the ship feels
[low voice, gruff] that's enough chatting, this mule needs feeding
[tired, dead tired] i'm done for the day. if she breaks again it can wait till i've slept
[warm, gentle tone] rest easy tonight. we earned it, her and me both
[warm, gentle tone] whole shift and not one tantrum out of her. who are you and what'd you do with my mule
[low voice, muttering] quiet like this in the yards meant the hull you were cutting still had secrets in it
[dry tone] this protein brick's tougher than the deck plate i cut yesterday. i'd know. i chewed both
[warm, gentle tone] hot food, warm engine, nobody bleeding. somebody frame this day
morning, you old cow. cough up your secrets before my coffee's done, i hate surprises after
[tired, dead tired] handing her over balanced and fed. wrote down every lie she told me today for the next shift
[grim, heavy] power's down to scraps. heat the people or light the halls â€” she can't haul both, so pick
[low voice, muttering] rationing amps like the last week of a salvage tow. hated it then, hate it now
[grim, heavy] scrubbers first. always scrubbers. you can fix anything in bad air except your own lungs
[PLEADING] come on, sweetheart, don't quit on me out here. that's how ships end up in yards like mine
[angry] a door that locks on its own crew is scrap wearing a uniform. i'll pull its actuator myself
[grim, heavy] cut a hundred doors open in the yards. never had somebody knocking from the other side before
[resigned, flat tone] computer's hurt and still trimming the power fair. that's a work ethic i can respect
[pained, voice tight] every wreck i ever stripped had a crew once. today i learned what that costs to know
[pained, voice tight] shoulder's grinding like a dry bearing. i'd swap the part out if anybody sold them for people
[panicked, panting] she's tearing at the seams and my hands are too small to hold a whole ship together
[warm, gentle tone] i keep a nameplate off every ship i ever stripped. box of ghosts under my bunk. they like the engine noise
batch under the aft coolant tank's about ready. it's medicinal, if the captain asks
[dry tone] port inspectors. thirty seconds aboard and they chalk a weld i'd hang my own weight off
[warm, gentle tone] Gus fell asleep mid-sentence about manifold torque again. didn't have the heart to say he'd told me twice already
up and at it. she behaved all night, which means she's saving something special for us
[low voice, gruff] sit. eat. the mule will still be broken when you're done chewing
[grim, heavy] captain, we're choosing what freezes tonight. i want your say before i start pulling breakers
[urgent, fast pace] air plant's wheezing like a hauled-up diver. i need spare hands and spare filters, that order
[suspicious tone] computer rerouted power without asking me. it's hurt, fine, but it better not make a habit of it
hey. you. eat something that isn't coffee, or i start telling people whose wrench keeps turning up in my toolbox
[pained, voice tight] come sit with me a minute. the dead don't need us tonight, but the living do
walk the cargo racks with me. something back there's ticking like a cheap clock and i want a witness
[dry tone] nothing's broken and nobody's bleeding, and it's making my teeth itch. talk to me about anything
[tired, dead tired] that's the shift. buy you a cup of whatever the mess is calling coffee
[warm, gentle tone] still upright, still greasy. day's young though
[laughing] tastes like the inside of a filter housing. eat it anyway, you'll want the ballast
[calm, measured] we've got amps to breathe and amps to think, in that order. the rest is comfort, and comfort keeps
[confident, assured tone] she's not dying, she's sulking. i've talked her out of blacker moods than this
[angry] don't defend that door to me. steel doesn't get to make choices about people
[suspicious tone] i've watched it too. it hesitates now, like a winch with a frayed line. you don't stand under that
[resigned, flat tone] fine, credit where it's due. the computer trims a power load better than my last two juniors
[warm, gentle tone] you don't carry it alone. that's the whole point of a crew, love
[low voice, gruff] quit walking on it and let the doc look. machines get downtime. so do you
[pained, voice tight] i've been dinged worse by a swinging hook. eyes on the gauge, not on me
[flirty, playful tone] keep sweet-talking me like that and i'll start thinking you want something torqued
[embarrassed, voice quiet] ah, hell. c'mere then, before i change my mind and marry the reactor instead
[laughing] that's grim. that's real grim. save it for my funeral, it'll land better
[curious tone] run that noise past me again. slow, like you're selling me a used ship
[panicked, panting] i hear it, i hear it â€” everybody out of the compartment, argue with me after
[warm, gentle tone] go on, get. me and the old cow have words to exchange in private
[tired, dead tired] enough. the mule can hold her grudges till morning, and so can i
[low voice, gruff] settled then. i'll bring the wrenches, you bring the nerve
[warm, gentle tone] we lived, she flew, and nobody i love is in the medbay tonight. good night
[dry tone] tell the captain the readings are boring again. best news this boat ever gets
suit's sealed. let's see what this rock has for us
[low voice, gruff] walking into a graveyard. another day at work
station's got our docking bay. let's not waste time
[low voice, gruff] their systems are probably worse than ours. always are
nothing yet. probably too good to last
so far so good. waiting for the other shoe to drop
[urgent, fast pace] things just got complicated out here
[nervous, speaking quickly] got a reading i don't like
[panicked, panting] everything's going to hell. get backup here now
[terrified, voice shaking] took damage. not dead yet though
[low voice, gruff] back in one piece. that's what matters
we made it. could've gone worse
[resigned, flat tone] saw some things out there. don't ask about them yet
[resigned, flat tone] we're back. just leave me alone for now
[low voice, gruff] got banged up but it's nothing fatal
[resigned, flat tone] took a hit. just need doc to patch it
[low voice, gruff] routine. i've done this a thousand times
[low voice, gruff] risky is just another word for interesting
[resigned, flat tone] grim doesn't begin to cover it. but we'll go anyway
[low voice, gruff] they're coming in. stay sharp
clamps hold. we're good
we're loose. heading out
running the preflight. same checks as always
[low voice, gruff] she's away clean. keeping an eye on it
[low voice, gruff] she's down safe. another one in the books
[low voice, gruff] something about the environment doesn't add up
[low voice, gruff] ship's being weird and i don't know why
[low voice, gruff] crew's acting off. makes me uneasy
[urgent, fast pace] we're under fire. this is not what i signed up for
[low voice, gruff] none of this makes any sense to me
